description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring a series of flat, rectangular solar panels mounted on a robust, metallic support structure. The panels are arranged in a linear configuration, elevated above a gridded base platform that appears to provide both structural support and ventilation. The surface of the panels is smooth and reflective, with a dark coloration typical of photovoltaic cells, while the base and mounting elements are composed of lighter metallic and yellow-toned materials. Several black mounting brackets and supports are visible along the edges, securing the panels in place. The overall design suggests a ground-mounted solar array suitable for urban environments such as rooftops, parking lots, or open public spaces. The structure is engineered for durability and efficient solar energy capture, with a slight tilt to optimize sunlight exposure.
description: A ground-mounted solar panel array with a metallic support structure and gridded base, featuring dark photovoltaic panels and yellow accent surfaces.
description_view_0: Front view showing the gridded base, yellow access platform, and the leading edge of the tilted solar panels.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the array, the tilt of the panels, and the extended yellow walkway along the base.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the panels, support brackets, and the continuation of the yellow platform.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the panel tilt, structural supports, and the full span of the gridded base.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 20 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly specified, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with metallic (likely aluminum or steel) frame and gridded base
attribute_array_configuration: single linear row of approximately 3-4 large panels
